disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Two weeks of escalating generalised fatigue, now profound enough to leave the patient bedbound and unable to do chores
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about the tiredness.
How has your energy been?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I've been getting more and more tired over the last couple of weeks and tonight it's so bad I can't get out of bed.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Nocturia four to five times per night, badly disrupting sleep
trigger_question_examples: How often are you getting up at night to pass urine?
Tell me about the night-time weeing.
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm up four or five times a night needing to pee and it's ruining my sleep.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Three-day history of a painful, spreading, red, itchy rash confined to the right inguinal skin fold
trigger_question_examples: Tell me about the rash.
Where exactly is the rash and how has it changed?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's a burning red rash in the crease of my groin that started three days ago and it's been spreading and getting more painful.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Gradual increase in central abdominal girth and body weight over the past year
trigger_question_examples: Has your weight changed recently?
Have you noticed any change in your waist size?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yeah, my belly and my waist have really grown over the past year.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Strong family history of type 2 diabetes; patient has never been formally tested
trigger_question_examples: Does anyone in your family have diabetes?
Have you ever had your blood sugar tested?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: There's loads of diabetes in my family — parents and siblings — but I've never had mine checked.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Dark, velvety, thickened skin in the neck and axillary folds and several small skin tags
trigger_question_examples: Can you show me the skin on your neck and under your arms on camera?
Have you noticed any darkening or thickening of the skin in your neck or armpit folds?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Now that you mention it, the skin in my neck folds and armpits has gone dark and a bit velvety, and I've got some little skin tags there too.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Home glucometer reading of 9.8 mmol/L taken about two hours after dinner
trigger_question_examples: Have you checked your blood sugar at all?
Do you have a glucose meter at home?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I checked on my home sugar meter after dinner and it read 9.8.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No burning or stinging on passing urine, no blood in the urine, no lower-abdominal pain
trigger_question_examples: Does it hurt or burn when you pass urine?
Have you seen any blood in your urine, or any pain low in your belly?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, it doesn't sting when I wee, there's no blood, and no pain in my belly — I just need to go a lot.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rash is limited to the opposing skin-fold surfaces with small satellite spots; no red streaks tracking away from it, not hot and hard beyond the fold
trigger_question_examples: Is the redness spreading in streaks away from the fold, or staying in the crease?
Are there smaller spots around the edge of the main rash?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's mostly in the crease where the skin touches, with a few little spots around the edge — there's no red line shooting down my leg.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No vomiting, no deep or rapid breathing, not drowsy or confused, able to keep fluids down
trigger_question_examples: Any vomiting, or are you breathing faster or deeper than usual?
Are you feeling drowsy, confused, or unable to keep drinks down?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I'm not throwing up, my breathing feels normal, I'm not confused, and I can drink OK — just exhausted.
